demo_request.note: Demo only — Notebook 04 uses per-station geocoded coordinates.

required_request_parameters.latitude: WGS84 latitude — obtained by geocoding eva in Notebook 04
required_request_parameters.longitude: WGS84 longitude — obtained by geocoding eva in Notebook 04
required_request_parameters.start_date: YYYY-MM-DD — match railway date range being processed
required_request_parameters.end_date: YYYY-MM-DD — match railway date range being processed
required_request_parameters.timezone: Europe/Berlin — aligns with German rail timestamps

response_top_level_keys.latitude: Grid-snapped latitude returned by API (may differ from request)

temperature_2m.description: Air temperature at 2 metres above ground.
temperature_2m.relevance: Extreme heat/cold may affect infrastructure and operations.

precipitation.description: Total precipitation (rain, showers, snow) in the preceding hour.
precipitation.relevance: Wet tracks, reduced adhesion, visibility issues.

rain.description: Liquid rain in the preceding hour.
rain.relevance: Component of total precipitation affecting operations.

snowfall.relevance: Winter disruption risk on ICE corridors.

windspeed_10m.description: Wind speed at 10 metres above ground.

weather_code.relevance: Encodes fog, thunderstorm, snow conditions in one field.

visibility.relevance: Fog and low visibility can cause speed restrictions.

hourly_time_format.merge_rule: Round railway departure_planned_time to nearest hour, then join on (latitude, longitude, hour)

railway_keys.time: departure_planned_time rounded to hour → Open-Meteo hourly.time

merge_plan.join_type: left join (keep all ICE railway rows; weather may be null if missing)

columns_not_in_weather_api: note: weather API is location+time only — no railway fields
